taxonomy: Isomacrolobium elongatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Dalbergieae. The plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Cameroon, the Central African Republic, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Isomacrolobium elongatum is a member of the genus Isomacrolobium, which is a part of the broader Fabaceae family. The classification of Isomacrolobium elongatum is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, subfamily Faboideae, tribe Dalbergieae, and genus Isomacrolobium.
